data.content:
### Shard Playwright Tests in GitLab CI with parallel keyword (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/ci.md

Demonstrates how to shard Playwright tests across multiple parallel jobs in GitLab CI using the 'parallel' keyword. This configuration splits tests into a specified number of smaller jobs, with each job using a specific shard index and total count.

```yaml
stages:
  - test

tests:
  stage: test
  image: mcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v%%VERSION%%-noble
  parallel: 7
  script:
    - npm ci
    - npx playwright test --shard=$CI_NODE_INDEX/$CI_NODE_TOTAL
```

--------------------------------

### Configure GitHub Actions workflow for parallel Playwright test sharding

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-sharding-js.md

Sets up a GitHub Actions job that runs Playwright tests in parallel across 4 shards using matrix strategy. Each shard executes a portion of the test suite and uploads its blob report as an artifact for later merging. The workflow triggers on push and pull request events to main/master branches.

```yaml
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  playwright-tests:
    timeout-minutes: 60
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        shardIndex: [1, 2, 3, 4]
        shardTotal: [4]
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v5
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
      with:
        node-version: lts/*
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci
    - name: Install Playwright browsers
      run: npx playwright install --with-deps

    - name: Run Playwright tests
      run: npx playwright test --shard=${{ matrix.shardIndex }}/${{ matrix.shardTotal }}

    - name: Upload blob report to GitHub Actions Artifacts
      if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: blob-report-${{ matrix.shardIndex }}
        path: blob-report
        retention-days: 1
```

--------------------------------

### Shard Playwright Tests in GitLab CI with parallel:matrix (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/ci.md

Shows how to use 'parallel:matrix' in GitLab CI to run Playwright tests with different project and shard combinations. This example creates multiple parallel jobs by combining various project values with several shard values, useful for comprehensive matrix testing.

```yaml
stages:
  - test

tests:
  stage: test
  image: mcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v%%VERSION%%-noble
  parallel:
    matrix:
      - PROJECT: ['chromium', 'webkit']
        SHARD: ['1/10', '2/10', '3/10', '4/10', '5/10', '6/10', '7/10', '8/10', '9/10', '10/10']
  script:
    - npm ci
    - npx playwright test --project=$PROJECT --shard=$SHARD
```

--------------------------------

### Shard Test Suite Execution Across Multiple Machines - Bash

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-sharding-js.md

Demonstrates how to split a Playwright test suite into four shards using the --shard command line option. Each shard runs one fourth of the tests independently, allowing parallel execution across different machines or CI jobs to achieve 4x faster test completion.

```bash
npx playwright test --shard=1/4
npx playwright test --shard=2/4
npx playwright test --shard=3/4
npx playwright test --shard=4/4
```

--------------------------------

### Azure Pipelines Sharded Test Execution Strategy for Playwright - JavaScript

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/ci.md

Advanced Azure Pipelines configuration implementing parallel test execution using sharding strategy. Defines a matrix strategy that distributes tests across three browsers (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit) with each browser split into three shards, resulting in nine parallel jobs for optimal test performance.

```yaml
trigger:
- main

pool:
  vmImage: ubuntu-latest

strategy:
  matrix:
    chromium-1:
      project: chromium
      shard: 1/3
    chromium-2:
      project: chromium
      shard: 2/3
    chromium-3:
      project: chromium
      shard: 3/3
    firefox-1:
      project: firefox
      shard: 1/3
    firefox-2:
      project: firefox
      shard: 2/3
    firefox-3:
      project: firefox
      shard: 3/3
    webkit-1:
      project: webkit
      shard: 1/3
    webkit-2:
      project: webkit
      shard: 2/3
    webkit-3:
      project: webkit
      shard: 3/3
steps:
- task: UseNode@1
  inputs:
    version: '22'
  displayName: 'Install Node.js'

- script: npm ci
  displayName: 'npm ci'
- script: npx playwright install --with-deps
  displayName: 'Install Playwright browsers'
- script: npx playwright test --project=$(project) --shard=$(shard)
  displayName: 'Run Playwright tests'
  env:
    CI: 'true'
```

--------------------------------

### TestConfig.shard

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-api/class-testconfig.md

Configures test sharding for parallel execution. Allows specifying the current shard and the total number of shards.

```APIDOC
## property: TestConfig.shard

### Description
Shard tests and execute only the selected shard. Specify in the one-based form like `{ total: 5, current: 2 }`. Learn more about [parallelism and sharding](../test-parallel.md) with Playwright Test.

### Method
Configuration Property

### Endpoint
N/A

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
N/A

#### Query Parameters
N/A

#### Request Body
- **current** (int) - Optional - The index of the shard to execute, one-based.
- **total** (int) - Optional - The total number of shards.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "shard": {
    "total": 10,
    "current": 3
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
N/A (Configuration Property)

#### Response Example
N/A
```

--------------------------------

### Shard Playwright Tests Across Multiple Machines

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-parallel-js.md

Shard Playwright tests to distribute execution across multiple machines. This command-line option specifies which shard of the test suite to run, useful for scaling test execution.

```bash
npx playwright test --shard=2/3
```

--------------------------------

### CircleCI Sharding Configuration for Playwright Tests in JavaScript

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/ci.md

CircleCI job configuration demonstrating test sharding with parallelism for JavaScript Playwright tests. Uses CIRCLE_NODE_INDEX incremented by 1 (since CircleCI uses 0-based indexing) to properly distribute tests across 4 parallel containers. This configuration ensures efficient test distribution while respecting CircleCI's indexing system.

```yaml
playwright-job-name:
  executor: pw-noble-development
  parallelism: 4
  steps:
    - run: SHARD="$((${CIRCLE_NODE_INDEX}+1))"; npx playwright test --shard=${SHARD}/${CIRCLE_NODE_TOTAL}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Test Sharding in Playwright

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-api/class-testconfig.md

Distribute tests across multiple shards for parallel execution using one-based indexing. Specify total number of shards and current shard index to execute only the designated portion of your test suite.

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
  shard: { total: 10, current: 3 },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Merge Playwright blob reports into single HTML report in GitHub Actions

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-sharding-js.md

Configures a dependent GitHub Actions job that runs after all test shards complete, downloading all blob report artifacts and merging them into a consolidated HTML report. The job executes even if some shards fail, and uploads the final HTML report with a 14-day retention period.

```yaml
jobs:
...
  merge-reports:
    # Merge reports after playwright-tests, even if some shards have failed
    if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
    needs: [playwright-tests]

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v5
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
      with:
        node-version: lts/*
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci

    - name: Download blob reports from GitHub Actions Artifacts
      uses: actions/download-artifact@v5
      with:
        path: all-blob-reports
        pattern: blob-report-*
        merge-multiple: true

    - name: Merge into HTML Report
      run: npx playwright merge-reports --reporter html ./all-blob-reports

    - name: Upload HTML report
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: html-report--attempt-${{ github.run_attempt }}
        path: playwright-report
        retention-days: 14
```

--------------------------------

### Merge Shard Reports into Single HTML Report - Bash

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-sharding-js.md

Command to merge blob reports from multiple test shards into a single consolidated HTML report. Requires all blob report files to be placed in a common directory, then uses the merge-reports command to generate a unified HTML report in the playwright-report directory.

```bash
npx playwright merge-reports --reporter html ./all-blob-reports
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Blob Reporter for CI Test Sharding - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-sharding-js.md

Configuration for enabling blob reporter in Playwright when running tests in CI environments. The blob reporter captures test results and attachments (traces, screenshots) that can later be merged from multiple shards into a unified report.

```typescript
export default defineConfig({
  testDir: './tests',
  reporter: process.env.CI ? 'blob' : 'html',
});
```

--------------------------------

### Configure xUnit Max Parallel Threads for Playwright .NET

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-runners-csharp.md

This snippet illustrates how to set the maximum number of parallel threads for xUnit tests when using Playwright for .NET. The xUnit.MaxParallelThreads parameter is passed via the dotnet test command to control test parallelism.

```bash
dotnet test -- xUnit.MaxParallelThreads=5
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright Pytest tests in parallel with pytest-xdist

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-runners-python.md

Install `pytest-xdist` to enable parallel execution of Playwright Pytest tests, significantly speeding up test suites on multi-core machines. The `--numprocesses auto` flag automatically determines the optimal number of parallel processes based on system resources.

```bash
pip install pytest-xdist
```

```bash
pytest --numprocesses auto
```

--------------------------------

### Shard Playwright test suite across multiple machines (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/best-practices-js.md

This command demonstrates how to shard a Playwright test suite, allowing it to be distributed and executed across multiple machines. The '--shard=1/3' argument specifies that this execution will run the first third of the entire test suite. This feature is crucial for scaling test execution in CI/CD environments by splitting the workload.

```bash
npx playwright test --shard=1/3
```

```bash
yarn playwright test --shard=1/3
```

```bash
pnpm exec playwright test --shard=1/3
```

--------------------------------

### Opt Out of Fully Parallel Mode in Playwright

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-parallel-js.md

Override the global `fullyParallel` setting in Playwright Test for specific describe blocks. Use `test.describe.configure({ mode: 'default' })` to ensure tests within that block run in the default sequential order, even if fully parallel mode is enabled globally.

```typescript
test.describe('runs in parallel with other describes', () => {
  test.describe.configure({ mode: 'default' });
  test('in order 1', async ({ page }) => {});
  test('in order 2', async ({ page }) => {});
});
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Fully Parallel Mode for Specific Playwright Projects

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-parallel-js.md

Configure a specific project within Playwright Test to run all its tests in parallel. This is done by setting `fullyParallel: true` within the project configuration in `playwright.config.ts`.

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
  // runs all tests in all files of a specific project in parallel
  projects: [
    {
      name: 'chromium',
      use: { ...devices['Desktop Chrome'] },
      fullyParallel: true,
    },
  ]
});
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright Test: Enable fully parallel test execution in config (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-js.md

This configuration snippet enables `fullyParallel` mode in Playwright Test. When set to `true`, tests within a single file will also run in parallel, in addition to the default parallel execution between files. This can significantly speed up test suites with many tests per file.

```js
export default {
  fullyParallel: true,
};
```

--------------------------------

### Configure JUnit 5 for Parallel Test Class Execution

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/junit-java.md

These properties configure JUnit Jupiter's parallel execution strategy. It enables parallel test execution, ensures methods within a single class run sequentially on the same thread, but runs different test classes concurrently. The 'dynamic' strategy uses 50% of available CPU cores for parallel threads.

```properties
junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.enabled = true
junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.mode.default = same_thread
junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.mode.classes.default = concurrent
junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.config.strategy=dynamic
junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.config.dynamic.factor=0.5
```

--------------------------------

### Run Playwright tests in parallel with Pytest

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/running-tests-python.md

Execute Playwright tests concurrently across multiple processes using Pytest, significantly speeding up test execution. This functionality requires the `pytest-xdist` plugin to be installed. The `--numprocesses` flag specifies the number of parallel workers to use.

```bash
pytest --numprocesses 2
```

--------------------------------

### Create Custom Fixture for Database User Isolation per Worker - JavaScript/TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-parallel-js.md

Defines a custom Playwright fixture that creates unique database users for each parallel worker using TestInfo.workerIndex. The fixture uses worker scope to ensure the same user is reused across all tests in a worker, with automatic cleanup after tests complete. This enables parallel test execution without data conflicts.

```javascript
import { test as baseTest, expect } from '@playwright/test';
// Import project utils for managing users in the test database.
import { createUserInTestDatabase, deleteUserFromTestDatabase } from './my-db-utils';

export * from '@playwright/test';
export const test = baseTest.extend<{}, { dbUserName: string }>({
  // Returns db user name unique for the worker.
  dbUserName: [async ({ }, use) => {
    // Use workerIndex as a unique identifier for each worker.
    const userName = `user-${test.info().workerIndex}`;
    // Initialize user in the database.
    await createUserInTestDatabase(userName);
    await use(userName);
    // Clean up after the tests are done.
    await deleteUserFromTestDatabase(userName);
  }, { scope: 'worker' }],
});
```

--------------------------------

### Limit Playwright Test Workers in Configuration

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-parallel-js.md

Configure the maximum number of parallel worker processes for Playwright Test within the `playwright.config.ts` file. This example sets a limit of 2 workers on CI environments and uses the default value locally.

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
  // Limit the number of workers on CI, use default locally
  workers: process.env.CI ? 2 : undefined,
});
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Fully Parallel Mode in Playwright Configuration

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-parallel-js.md

Enable fully parallel mode for all tests in all files within the Playwright Test configuration file (`playwright.config.ts`). This setting ensures that all tests are executed in parallel across available workers.

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
  fullyParallel: true,
});
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright tests to run in parallel mode

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-api/class-test.md

This snippet shows how to set the execution mode for all tests in a file or enclosing scope to `parallel` using `test.describe.configure`. This causes tests to run concurrently using parallel workers, improving execution speed.

```javascript
// Run all the tests in the file concurrently using parallel workers.
test.describe.configure({ mode: 'parallel' });
test('runs in parallel 1', async ({ page }) => {});
test('runs in parallel 2', async ({ page }) => {});
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Multiple Web Servers in Playwright

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-webserver-js.md

Launch multiple web servers (e.g., frontend and backend) simultaneously by providing an array of webServer configurations. Each server configuration includes command, URL, name, timeout, and server reuse settings. This is useful for testing applications that depend on multiple services running together.

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
  webServer: [
    {
      command: 'npm run start',
      url: 'http://localhost:3000',
      name: 'Frontend',
      timeout: 120 * 1000,
      reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI,
    },
    {
      command: 'npm run backend',
      url: 'http://localhost:3333',
      name: 'Backend',
      timeout: 120 * 1000,
      reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI,
    }
  ],
  use: {
    baseURL: 'http://localhost:3000',
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright webServer wait with stdout regex

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-js.md

This configuration snippet for Playwright's `webServer` option demonstrates how to make Playwright wait until the webserver's stdout matches a specific regular expression. It also shows how to use a named capture group in the regex to extract a value, such as a port number, which Playwright then makes available as an environment variable for subsequent tests. This is useful for services that print readiness messages to stdout/stderr instead of offering an HTTP readiness check.

```js
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
  webServer: {
    command: 'npm run start',
    wait: {
      stdout: '/Listening on port (?<my_server_port>\d+)/'
    }
  }
});
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright with environment tags for multi-environment testing

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-sharding-js.md

Demonstrates configuring Playwright to use environment-specific tags that are automatically included in blob reports. This configuration uses blob reporter in CI environments and HTML reporter locally, while tagging all tests with the CI environment name for differentiation when merging reports from multiple environments.

```javascript
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
  reporter: process.env.CI ? 'blob' : 'html',
  tag: process.env.CI_ENVIRONMENT_NAME,  // for example "@APIv2"
});
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright Test Fixtures for Parallel JUnit 5

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-runners-java.md

This Java example sets up Playwright for parallel test execution with JUnit 5. It uses `@TestInstance(TestInstance.Lifecycle.PER_CLASS)` to create Playwright and Browser instances once per test class, ensuring thread safety. `@BeforeAll` and `@AfterAll` manage browser lifecycle, while `@BeforeEach` and `@AfterEach` handle thread-specific `BrowserContext` and `Page` objects for individual test methods.

```java
// Subclasses will inherit PER_CLASS behavior.
@TestInstance(TestInstance.Lifecycle.PER_CLASS)
class TestFixtures {
  // Shared between all tests in the class.
  Playwright playwright;
  Browser browser;

  @BeforeAll
  void launchBrowser() {
    playwright = Playwright.create();
    browser = playwright.chromium().launch();
  }

  @AfterAll
  void closeBrowser() {
    playwright.close();
  }

  // New instance for each test method.
  BrowserContext context;
  Page page;

  @BeforeEach
  void createContextAndPage() {
    context = browser.newContext();
    page = context.newPage();
  }

  @AfterEach
  void closeContext() {
    context.close();
  }
}

class Test1 extends TestFixtures {
  @Test
  void shouldClickButton() {
    page.navigate("data:text/html,<script>var result;</script><button onclick='result=\"Clicked\"'>Go</button>");
    page.locator("button").click();
    assertEquals("Clicked", page.evaluate("result"));
  }

  @Test
  void shouldCheckTheBox() {
    page.setContent("<input id='checkbox' type='checkbox'></input>");
    page.locator("input").check();
    assertTrue((Boolean) page.evaluate("() => window['checkbox'].checked"));
  }

  @Test
  void shouldSearchWiki() {
    page.navigate("https://www.wikipedia.org/");
    page.locator("input[name=\"search\"]").click();
    page.locator("input[name=\"search\"]").fill("playwright");
    page.locator("input[name=\"search\"]").press("Enter");
    assertEquals("https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Playwright", page.url());
  }
}

class Test2 extends TestFixtures {
  @Test
  void shouldReturnInnerHTML() {
    page.setContent("<div>hello</div>");
    assertEquals("hello", page.innerHTML("css=div"));
  }

  @Test
  void shouldClickButton() {
    Page popup = page.waitForPopup(() -> {
      page.evaluate("window.open('about:blank');");
    });
    assertEquals("about:blank", popup.url());
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Merge Playwright Reports with Multiple Reporters (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-sharding-js.md

This command merges Playwright blob reports from a specified directory and generates both HTML and GitHub reports. The `--reporter` option allows defining one or more output formats for the consolidated report, separated by commas.

```bash
npx playwright merge-reports --reporter=html,github ./blob-reports
```

--------------------------------

### Configure NUnit Parallel Test Workers for Playwright .NET

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-runners-csharp.md

This snippet demonstrates how to adjust the number of worker processes for parallel test execution when using NUnit with Playwright for .NET. It utilizes the NUnit.NumberOfTestWorkers parameter passed to the dotnet test command to control concurrency.

```bash
dotnet test -- NUnit.NumberOfTestWorkers=5
```

--------------------------------

### Limit Playwright Test Workers via Command Line

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-parallel-js.md

This command allows you to specify the maximum number of parallel worker processes for Playwright Test execution directly from the command line. This is useful for controlling resource usage during test runs.

```bash
npx playwright test --workers 4
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Multiple Web Servers in Playwright Test (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/release-notes-js.md

Demonstrates how to configure Playwright to launch and manage multiple web servers concurrently using the `webServer` array in `playwright.config.ts`. This allows testing applications with separate frontend and backend services, ensuring all necessary processes are running before tests execute.

```js
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';
export default defineConfig({
  webServer: [
    {
      command: 'npm run start',
      url: 'http://127.0.0.1:3000',
      timeout: 120 * 1000,
      reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI,
    },
    {
      command: 'npm run backend',
      url: 'http://127.00.1:3333',
      timeout: 120 * 1000,
      reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI,
    }
  ],
  use: {
    baseURL: 'http://localhost:3000/',
  },
});
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright for Sequential Test Execution with Test List - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-parallel-js.md

Configures Playwright to disable parallel execution and use a specific test list file. Sets workers to 1 to ensure sequential test execution and specifies testMatch to only run the test list file. This configuration is required for the test list pattern to control execution order properly.

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
  workers: 1,
  testMatch: 'test.list.ts',
});
```
